headlines.title: Katz: Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon even if US demands it
headlines.snippet: Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon, even if the United States demands it. Israel will maintain its presence in the country.

headlines.title: Palestinian activist faints after his release from Israeli prison
headlines.snippet: Palestinian activist Abdullah Shatat collapsed immediately after his release from an Israeli jail.

headlines.title: Family sues Tesla for wrongful death in Autopilot crash in Texas, US
headlines.snippet: A family has filed a lawsuit against Tesla in Texas, alleging that the company's Autopilot system was responsible for a fatal crash. They are seeking $1 million in damages.

headlines.title: Zimbabwe’s Senate approves amendment extending presidential term
headlines.snippet: Zimbabwe's Senate has approved a constitutional amendment that will extend the presidential term. This amendment will allow President Mnangagwa to remain in office until 2030.

headlines.title: US public health agency concludes hantavirus response as outbreak eases
headlines.snippet: The US Department of Health and Human Services has announced the conclusion of its hantavirus response efforts, coinciding with a decrease in the outbreak.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: Donald Trump expressed skepticism regarding the United States' responsibility for a deadly strike on an Iranian primary school. He stated he had seen no evidence implicating the US.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance indicated that the US is reviewing the conditions for Turkey's potential purchase of F-35 fighter jets. The review will determine if Turkey has met the legal requirements.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) confirmed that UN nuclear inspectors will proceed with inspections in Iran. This will occur despite concerns from Tehran regarding timing.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it struck two people in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military reported striking two individuals in southern Lebanon. The military stated they were in a vehicle and posed a threat, but did not provide evidence.

headlines.title: US says 20 million barrels of crude passed through Hormuz in past day
headlines.snippet: Approximately 20 million barrels of crude oil transited the Strait of Hormuz in the past day, according to US Energy Secretary Chris Wright. Iranian mines were cited as a factor slowing shipping.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued a ruling preventing Donald Trump from implementing a proof of citizenship requirement for voting.

headlines.title: US-Iran deal may leave Netanyahu as biggest casualty
headlines.snippet: A deal between the US and Iran aimed at ending the Middle East war could potentially have significant repercussions for Netanyahu.

headlines.title: Italy slams NATO chief’s comments on Iran war flights
headlines.snippet: Italy has officially refuted comments made by the NATO chief regarding flights over Iran. Italy stated it did not permit the use of American bases for strikes against Iran.

headlines.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills mine disposal expert: NGO
headlines.snippet: A Russian strike in Ukraine resulted in the death of a mine disposal expert, according to an NGO.

headlines.title: Erdogan says bilateral talks with Trump likely at NATO summit in Turkey
headlines.snippet: Erdogan indicated that bilateral discussions with Trump are likely to take place during the NATO summit in Turkey.

headlines.title: UK government's answer to stolen Palestinian land? Ad watchdog
headlines.snippet: The UK government's response to the sale of illegal Israeli settlement homes is being criticized as insufficient. Sanctions are highlighted as weak in addressing the issue.

headlines.title: Syria trial opens for Assad cousin accused of war crimes
headlines.snippet: A Syrian trial has commenced for a cousin of Assad, who stands accused of war crimes. The court is considering acts attributed to Wassim al-Assad as crimes against humanity.

headlines.title: US to progress with Turkey jet engine sales ahead of NATO summit
headlines.snippet: The United States is moving forward with plans to sell jet engines to Turkey. This development precedes the upcoming NATO summit.

headlines.title: Yemen rejects Israeli claims of Houthi plot against Eilat
headlines.snippet: Yemen has denied Israeli assertions regarding a supposed Houthi plot against Eilat. Yemeni officials refuted reports of Yemenia Airways' involvement in an alleged attack plan.

headlines.title: Hunger and desperation grip Sudan's El-Obeid as RSF closes in
headlines.snippet: A humanitarian crisis is developing in Sudan's El-Obeid as RSF paramilitaries approach the city. There are fears of atrocities if the city is captured.

headlines.title: كيف تستعد طهران لإحياء مراسم يوم العاشر من محرم؟
headlines.snippet: Tehran is preparing for the commemoration of Ashura, the tenth day of Muharram.

headlines.snippet: Internal turmoil in Israel is exacerbating the crisis and impacting external relations. The situation within Israel is detailed.
